Anyone who doesn't have this movie top rated just doesn't get movies....or life. This movie is the perfect sequel. It gets it. Delivers what all fans of the original could hope for and more. Was diverse but didnt feel woke. It was predictable yet unexpected. It gave you everything. Tears, laughs, thrills, drama, reunion, and callbacks to the original were amazing. Penny Benjamin....excellent delivery from the story of the flybys. Rooster played the emotional son of Goose and he delivered. Hangman was excellent and what you want in a pilot and leading man. Reading some of the critics you can tell who you never want to trust for a review again. This movie is 100% in my book. I watched it 5 times and every time I cry, pump my fist, laugh, and cheer. Praying for a 3rd. But how to top it?

Unrespectable Military-Entertainment Complex and Mediocre Acting. So they start the movie off by destroying a 350 Million Dollar jet for fun, paid for by tax payer money. The actors seem like theatre kids playing dress up. The US Military has their fingerprints all over the script. Its just all so flashy and glamorous. Gimme a break. I don't think this stuff is supposed to be taken as lightly as they portray in the movie. Ive never left a review for a movie before but this was pretty stupid and made me cringe and kinda angry. Holy shit, fuck whoever made this movie, stupid idiots!!!!! At the end, why wouldn't they wait in the forest for an apache to come pick them up? Yeah after the mission is over lets run on to the destroyed enemy airstrip and steal some old plane that we don't even know works and take on the 5th gen fighters, some bullshit straight out of hollywood. What a dumbass movie. Shame on the writers and US Military.

Perfect movie and a must rewatch. Very rarely do sequels outshine the original, and are often argued whether they did or not for many years amongst peers. I thoroughly enjoyed the original as you may have guessed by my username, so I was hesitant going in. TopGun Maverick knocked it out of the park and then some. The story, directing, acting, sfx, music, everything is 10/10. The lengths they went for the action pieces in actual fighter jets, the focus on practical effects and not cgi, win big points. A big win for cinema.

masterpiece of a movie, amazing cast and storyline. This is a movie that if you see on tv or someone is watching it, you get wheeled in to watch it too. This movie goes above and beyond expectations. Sounds effects are amazing with a home theater and is a must watch for everyone.

I would give this movie negative stars if it were possible. It makes me genuinely question my sanity when I see movies like this come out with 90+ percent ratings. This movie relies so heavily on nostalgia that you can smell it in the room, and I suppose that is supposed to make up for the insanely bad writing, acting, and storyline? How many times can we see the same ideas pushed into these blockbuster films that have been regurgitated over and and over, and then just because it gives us a bit of action and a love story they still get away with it and make their money? I mean let's be real, they were going to make a profit on this film no matter what simply by attaching the name "Top Gun" to the title. Please stop making garbage films like this.
Liked the original and wondered how the sequel would compare…. Seen it a few times now and I enjoy it just as much each time! Fantastic film with a great plot

My favorite movie of 2022. the film is packed with breathtaking aerial cinematography that makes you feel like you’re right in the cockpit. The practical flight sequences are jaw-dropping, immersive, and unlike anything seen in modern action films. Top Gun Maverick is pure cinematic adrenaline and a reminder of why movies are meant to be experienced on the big screen.

A rare bit of nostalgia that may even surpass the original. Kosinski puts together something that is both grand, beautiful, and genuine. It is rare in this day and age to see a Hollywood blockbuster-style film, let alone a sequel with a 36-year gap that shows such tender loving care for the source material. And last but not least...."Thank you for saving my life." "That's what my dad would have done." That is when men cried.

Greetings from Lithuania. I wasn't the biggest fan of first "Top Gun" movie. I saw it like 5 years ago so for me it wasn't nostalic or anything. It was just OK movie and nothing more. Today i saw "Top Gun: Maverick" (2022) in cinema. And it blew me away. This movie was incredible. It literally put a smile on my face. Action scenes in the air were the best i ever saw of this type of action. Directing by Joseph Kosinski was perfect. Music was on spot. Cinematography was gorgeous. I loved how actually calm and quite this movie was, until it wasn't and transition between scenes, acts, calm and action was perfect. Acting was great. This is a movie making as good as it gets. Was it predictable? Yes. But at the same time i was excited and glued to the screen during its whole 2 hours run time. Overall, "Top Gun: Maverick" is a Hollywood blockbuster done right. Its a Hollywood action adventure movie in every possible way, yet this time its done incredibly good. What a terrific sequel.
